sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off oggi, con il VIX a 24.54 (-2.81%) e il DXY a 100.08 (+0.43%). Il prezzo del petrolio è salito a $108.08 (+6.84%) e il TLT è sceso a 86.26 (-0.50%). La guerra tra Israele e Iran continua a pesare sui mercati, con il Brent che registra un aumento significativo.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is risk-off today, with VIX at 24.54 (-2.81%) and DXY at 100.08 (+0.43%). Oil prices surged to $108.08 (+6.84%) and TLT fell to 86.26 (-0.50%). The ongoing conflict between Israel and Iran continues to weigh on markets, with Brent crude seeing a significant increase.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario europeo (STOXX 600) è salito del 2.38%, mentre l'S&P 500 ha guadagnato lo 0.72%. Il VIX è sceso sotto i 25 punti, ma rimane elevato. Il rendimento del decennale americano è salito al 4.31%. Il prezzo dell'oro è sceso del 3.41% a $4620.20/oz. Il Bitcoin è sceso del 2.17% a $66,600.
market_impact_summary.en: European stocks (STOXX 600) rose 2.38%, while S&P 500 gained 0.72%. VIX fell below 25 but remains elevated. US 10-year yield rose to 4.31%. Gold price dropped 3.41% to $4620.20/oz. Bitcoin fell 2.17% to $66,600.

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno e benvenuti all'aggiornamento finanziario di oggi, il vostro Morning Briefing quotidiano.

La seduta asiatica ci consegna un Nikkei in calo del 2.38 percento a 52,463 punti e Shanghai in perdita dello 0.84 percento a 3915.53 punti. Questi dati anticipano una possibile apertura cauta in Europa.

Il sentiment di mercato è decisamente risk-off, come evidenziato dal calo dei mercati azionari e dal ritorno alla sicurezza dei bond. L'S&P 500 ieri ha chiuso in positivo, +0.72% a 6575.32, ma il VIX, indice della paura, scende al 24.54, -2.81%. Il Dollar Index (DXY) sale dello 0.43% a 100.08, mentre l'euro/dollaro scambia a 1.15, -0.32%. I tassi di interesse continuano a salire, con il decennale Usa al 4.31%, +0.19%. L'oro, bene rifugio per eccellenza, perde il 3.41% a 4620 dollari l'oncia.

La situazione geopolitica rimane tesa, con l'intensificarsi dei rischi per il sistema finanziario legati alla situazione in Iran, secondo la Banca d'Inghilterra. Il governo cinese continua a vedere i suoi bond come un rifugio sicuro.

Sul fronte macroeconomico, l'attenzione si concentra sui prossimi dati in uscita. Negli Usa, il PIL del primo trimestre sarà pubblicato il 29 aprile. La BCE ha lasciato invariato il tasso di interesse al 2.00% nella riunione di ieri. L'inflazione nella zona euro sarà attentamente monitorata con la prossima uscita dei dati il 2 aprile.

Il mercato delle criptovalute continua a mostrare debolezza, con Bitcoin che scambia a 66,600 dollari, -2.17%.

In conclusione, l'outlook per oggi sembra essere cauto, con gli investitori che monitorano da vicino l'evoluzione della situazione geopolitica e i prossimi dati economici. Sarà importante osservare come apriranno i mercati europei e americani oggi.

Spostiamo l'attenzione sul mercato delle criptovalute, dove abbiamo visto movimenti interessanti nelle ultime ore.

Bitcoin è sceso del 3.49% arrivando a $66,611. Gli ETF Bitcoin hanno registrato un afflusso di $117.5M. Questo dato positivo potrebbe indicare una certa fiducia degli investitori nel mercato.

Ethereum è scesa del 4.70% a $2,048, mentre Solana ha perso il 6.41% arrivando a $79.06. Binance Coin (BNB) è scesa del 4.96% a $589.31. Questi movimenti sono in linea con la tendenza generale di mercato.

L'indice Fear & Greed è a 12, livello di Estrema Paura. Questo indice misura il sentiment del mercato, e un valore basso come questo potrebbe indicare una buona opportunità di acquisto per gli investitori più esperti. Tuttavia, è importante tenere conto anche dei dati macroeconomici che influenzano il mercato.

In generale, il mercato delle criptovalute sembra essere in una fase di consolidamento. Gli investitori stanno attendendo ulteriori dati macroeconomici per decidere la prossima mossa. La situazione geopolitica e le decisioni delle banche centrali continueranno a influenzare il mercato.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is cautious, with risk-off mood prevailing. Key data points show that the US Core CPI is at 2.7% Year over Year, and Non-Farm Payrolls came in at -92,000. These figures are influencing market movements.

In traditional markets, equities saw a mixed close. The S&P 500 ended at 6,575.32, up 0.72%. The STOXX 600 rose to 59.29, up 2.38%. The NIKKEI closed at 52,463, down 2.38%. In currencies, the Euro to US Dollar exchange rate is at 1.15, down 0.32%. The Dollar Index is at 100.08, up 0.43%. Commodities saw Brent crude rise to $108.08, up 6.84%, while Gold fell to $4,620.20 per ounce, down 3.41%. US 10-year yields are at 4.31%, up 0.19%, and TLT Bond USA 20Y is at 86.26, down 0.50%.

Geopolitically, the situation remains tense. The Iranian conflict is escalating, and its impact on the financial system is being closely monitored. The Bank of England has warned of intensifying risks. In economic news, the European Central Bank recently kept rates steady at 2.00%. China's GDP growth is expected to have accelerated in Q1 despite the Iran shock. The US Federal Reserve kept the Fed Funds rate at 3.64%. The global M2 liquidity proxy is at $22.7 trillion, expanding with a US Year over Year increase of 4.9%.

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a closer look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

Starting with Bitcoin, we see that the daily net inflow into BTC ETFs was $117.5 million. From a technical standpoint, Bitcoin is currently trading at $66,611, down 3.49% from the previous day. The flows into ETFs suggest that investors are still showing interest in the asset, but the price action indicates a cautious approach.

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the major altcoins. Ethereum, Solana, and Binance Coin are all experiencing declines, with ETH down 4.70% to $2,048, SOL down 6.41% to $79.06, and BNB down 4.96% to $589.31. Despite these short-term price movements, these assets continue to hold significant positions in the market.

In terms of sentiment and fear/greed, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 12, indicating extreme fear. This is often a sign that investors are overly cautious, which can lead to potential buying opportunities. However, it's essential to consider the broader market context, including macroeconomic factors and correlation with traditional assets. The current market correlation and index analysis suggest that investors are becoming increasingly risk-averse, which is reflected in the declines across various asset classes.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you in the next episode.

articles.title: Why Europe Is Unlikely to Face an Inflation Surge
articles.snippet: In 2021-22, the European Central Bank failed to pay sufficient heed to mounting inflationary pressures, leading to a delayed response that made matters much worse. While today’s energy-price shock remains less acute than the one caused by Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine, the ECB appears committed to a more proactive response.

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…

articles.title: China’s AI Is Spreading Fast. Here’s How to Stop the Security Risks
articles.snippet: In late 2024, Chinese models accounted for one percent of global AI workloads. By the end of 2025, that figure had surged to 30 percent. Alibaba’s Qwen family now boasts over 700 million downloads, making it the world’s largest provider of “open-source” AI systems that are publicly released and capable of being downloaded and run locally. A constellation of Chinese AI labs — DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax chief among them — are increasingly popular fixtures of a global, open-source marketplace, which is starting to power everything from Indian academic research to America’s most elite technology startups.Though they are open-weight and The post China’s AI Is Spreading Fast. Here’s How to Stop the Security Risks appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: The Arsenal as the Battlefield: The War on Iran and the Return of Counter-Industrial Targeting
articles.snippet: America’s war against Iran has sparked heated debates over U.S. strategic priorities, military objectives, and defense industrial capacity. It has also fueled speculation about how a hypothetical clash between the United States and the People’s Republic of China might unfold. Tehran’s ability to launch salvo after salvo of simple attack drones reflects current thinking about how the proliferation of cheap, easily producible precision weapons is changing the character of war, and previews some of the challenges that Washington might confront in a fight with Beijing. Facing an even bigger and better-armed rival, the United States is likely to find that The post The Arsenal as the Battlefield: The War on Iran and the Return of Counter-Industrial Targeting appeared first on War on the Rocks .

Ellis wrote, “Trump Needs a Plan on Ukraine’s Buried Treasure,” where he argued that Washington should enact a strategy for Ukraine that ensures Ukrainian control and allied access to its storehouse of critical minerals. articles.title: Record Wind Output Fails to Stop UK Energy Price Surge
articles.snippet: The UK cannot wind power itself out of the new energy crisis resulting from the war in the Middle East. With about a third of its electricity generation typically coming from gas-fired power plants, the UK is feeling the energy shock after oil and gas prices soared in the month since the war in Iran began. And the UK is likely to feel the worst of the energy price shock, according to the International Monetary Fund (IMF). That’s despite the record wind power generation in the first quarter of 2026 and the new daily record in wind power output…

articles.title: Europe's Nuclear U-Turn: What's Changed, What Hasn't, and What's Next
articles.snippet: Nuclear energy is once again at the center of fierce debate between European leaders as a new energy crisis sweeps the world, leaving the import-dependent European Union scrambling for alternative energy sources. The bloc still imports more than half of its energy needs, leaving it extremely vulnerable to global market shocks, like the unprecedented interruption to oil and gas supplies currently taking place in the Strait of Hormuz as the United States and Israel continue to wage war with Iran. In order to keep the lights on and prevent huge numbers…

articles.title: Dubai Benchmark Pressured by Hormuz Disruptions
articles.snippet: The Iran war and effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has stranded the majority of Middle East oil exports, putting the region’s key oil benchmark under a lot of pressure. The Dubai crude benchmark--a vital marker for Middle Eastern oil flowing to Asia--used to price ~18 million barrels per day primarily based on crude oil grades from the United Arab Emirates (specifically Upper Zakum and Murban), Oman and Qatar. According to Reuters, three of the five crude grades that normally underpin the benchmark are now effectively sidelined…
articles.title_it: Dubai Benchmark Pressured by Hormuz Disruptions

articles.title: Trump’s Renewed Iran Threats Drive Oil Higher
articles.snippet: Crude oil price moved higher today, adding some 5% from Wednesday, following President Donald Trump’s address to the nation, in which he said the United States would continue attacking Iran. Brent crude was trading at $107.49 per barrel at the time of writing, up by over 6% from Wednesday. WTI was up by over 5%, trading at $105.25 per barrel. The U.S. president added that the army had come close to accomplishing its goals in the war, saying that “We are going to finish the job, and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very…

articles.title: Oil Prices Surge 6% as Trump Signals Prolonged Iran Conflict
articles.snippet: Oil prices surged in early Asian trade on Thursday after President Trump signaled that Washington would continue its military campaign against Iran, including potential strikes on energy infrastructure. Before the speech, prices had dropped on expectations of de-escalation, leading to a sharp spike when Trump made it clear the war would continue. At the time of writing, WTI was trading at $105.2, up 5.07%, while Brent stood at $107.3, up 6.04% Before the speech, prices had dropped on expectations of de-escalation, leading to a sharp spike when…

articles.summary_en: Oil prices surged in early Asian trade on Thursday after President Trump signaled that Washington would continue its military campaign against Iran, including potential strikes on energy infrastructure. Before the speech, prices had dropped on expectations of de-escalation, leading to a sharp spike when Trump made it clear the war would continue. At the time of writing, WTI was trading at $105.2, up 5.07%, while Brent stood at $107.3, up 6.04% Before the speech, prices had dropped on expectations of de-escalation, leading to a sharp spike when…

articles.title: Bitcoin dips, oil rises as Trump addresses nation on war in Iran
articles.snippet: US President Donald Trump said the military is "very close" to finishing its war in Iran in a speech that saw oil and Bitcoin prices move.

articles.title: CoinShares stock makes US debut on Nasdaq following SPAC merger
articles.snippet: Shares of the European crypto asset manager are set to start trading today, marking a venue shift as digital asset companies navigate a weaker market backdrop.
